[10.] ► *Most extreme despondency, [27] . ► Anxiety in the head, with internal and external warmth of the forehead, and unchanged pulse, disappearing in the open air (after one hour and a half), [15] . ► A kind of oppression which puts him in an anxious mood; he is unable to work; he must leave the room and go into the open air; the depression does not leave him the whole day, [13] . ► Great anxiety about trifles, in the evening in bed; he is unable to fall asleep, [13] . ► Out of humor, sad, for a short time (after one hour), [14] . ► Fretfulness (after 20 drops), [5] . ► Everything frets him (after one hour), [15] . ► Peevish, fretful mood, [38] . ► Extremely peevish and ill-humored, [37] . ► He is vexed and fretful about everything, at 2 P.M., [14] . ► Intellectual. [70.] ► Headache, sometimes worse in the forehead, sometimes in the occiput; most severe and persistent over the orbits; scarcely felt so long as he remained in the open air, but it became very violent when going into a hot room, where he felt badly and exhausted (after 15 drops), [5] . ► Headache, as after intoxication (after one hour and a half), [4] . ► Headache, as if dull and heavy, disappearing during sleep (first evening), [14] . ► Dull and violently pressive headache (soon after 10 drops), [11] . ► Boring headache, immediately (after 20 drops), [5] . ► The head is pressed downward, relieved by becoming erect (after a quarter of an hour), [14] . ► Feeling as though the brain were pressed against the upper and forepart of the skull, especially in the region of the forehead, with great pressure towards the orbits (after 14 drops), [12] . ► Pressive headache extending over the whole head (after 30 drops), [5] . ► Pressive headache, in the morning in bed, [37] . ► Headache alternately in the forehead and occiput, pressive; gradually disappearing after half an hour, during which the pulse was diminished about 12 beats (after 10 drops), [5] . [80.] ► Sticking here and there in the head, at 4 P.M., [13] . ► A kind of sticking and crawling in the brain, violent and long-lasting, [13] . ► Stitching, extending from the right lower jaw through the head by the right ear (after one hour and a half), [14] . ► Headache, tearing in the evening in bed, [14] . ► Throbbing headache, worse on stooping, in the left side (after one hour), [13] . ► Jerkings at times in the head, back, and cervical muscles, followed by paralysis, [23] . ► Forehead. ► Feeling of warmth in the middle of the forehead , *then a coolness as from a draft of air, the latter continuing a long time (after half an hour), [14] . ► Feeling of heaviness in the forehead, on going out of the room (after one hour), [14] . ► Heaviness in the forehead, and pressure in the orbits (after 112 drops), [4] . ► Heaviness and stupefying pain in the forehead, on stooping, not disappearing on rising (after one hour), [14] . [90.] ► Pain in the right side of the forehead, [13] . ► Pain in the left side of the forehead, on stooping low down, with a sensation as though the brain fell forward, disappearing after rising (after half an hour), [14] . ► Stupefying pain in the forehead (after one hour), [14] . ► Feeling of fulness in the forehead (after half an hour), [14] . ► Tensive sensation in the forehead and left side of the face (after half an hour), [14] . ► Violent boring headache above the orbits (after 25 drops), [5] . ► A drawing sensation in the middle of the forehead (after half an hour), [14] . ► Twinging pain in the forehead, slight but long-lasting; with sleepiness (after one hour), [13] . ► Pressure in the frontal region, with slow pulse (after 25 drops), [6] . ► Pressure commences in the forehead, in the open air, becomes worse in the house, with great heat in the face, without thirst; the headache is relieved by rest, [37] . Stupefaction, with falling down, and loss of consciousness. Feeling of intoxication and vertigo, with drowsiness. Vertigo, on rising from a stooping posture, or on getting up from a seat, with what appears to be a veil before the sight, or with a sensation as if all objects were turning round. Vertigo in the open air. Stupefying pain in the whole head. Stupefying headache, with a feeling of weight, and sensation, on stooping, as if the brain fell forwards and struck against the cranium. Brain feels contracted and painful. Stitches in head. Itching of hairy scalp. Very violent pressive headache in a room. Constant feeling of coldness in head. Sensation of coldness in forehead and vertex, as if a cold wind were blowing on it, descending through neck to back; in room, > in open air. Feeling as if ice lay on vertex. Congestion in the head, with heat and throbbing. Pulsation in head, with heat or with coldness. The headache disappears, with a sensation of coldness in vertex, forehead, in nape of neck, extending to loins. Headache in r. temple.

Coolness on forehead as from a draft of air. Periodical paroxysms of aching pain under frontal bone. Boring headache, particularly above eyelids. Oppression on top of head, as from a weight. Beating in head, < stooping. Stupefying pain in whole head. Pulsation in head, with heat or coldness. Stitches in head. Headache : > after eating ; lacerating, in evening in bed ; from nervous prostration, with dragging pain, accompanied by pain and swelling in hypochondrium and yellow spots on face. Sensation of looseness of brain, as if it were falling into forehead when stooping, without pain. The brain feels contracted and painful. Sensation of coldness in forehead and vertex, as if a cold wind was blowing on it, descending through neck to back ; < in room, > in open air. Itching of hairy scalp.

(second day), [14] . ► The secretion of mucus in the nose is suppressed, without the slightest sensation of catarrh, [37] . [240.] ► The catarrh becomes loosened (after two hours), [14] . ► Stopped catarrh, on stooping, can get no air through the nose, in the morning (second day), [14] . ► Subjective. ► Stoppage of the nose, as if coryza would come on, at 5 P.M., [14] . ► Stoppage of the nose, she could scarcely get any air in (after three-quarters of an hour); can get no air through the nose (after one hour and a half); the stoppage of the nose loosens (after two hours), [14] . ► Feeling of coldness as from ice near both wings of the nose, [37] . ► Feeling in the nose as though catarrh would appear (after one hour and a half), [14] . ► Feeling above the nose as in catarrh (third hour), [14] . ► Feeling of fulness in the nose (after two hours and a half), [14] . ► Cramplike pain in the right nasal bone (after a quarter of an hour), [37] . ► Drawing extending up through the nose (after one hour and a half), [14] . [250.] ► A pressing-forward sensation in the upper nasal region (after one hour), [14] . ► Pressing-asunder pain in the nasal bones, [37] . ► At one time sticking, at another pressing-inward in the root of the nose (after three-quarters of an hour), [14] . ► Fine stitches externally in the right wing of the nose, at 2.30 P.M., [14] . ► Frequent slight crawling and itching on the left outer wing of the nose, which, when touched on the inside, was painful as if ulcerated, [37] . ► Tickling in the right nostril (after two hours), [14] . ► Itching in the upper part of the nose (after three-quarters of an hour), [14] . ► Itching of the left wing of the nose (after three-quarters of an hour), [14] . ► Itching in both nostrils disappearing on rubbing (after two hours and three-quarters), [14] . ► Violent itching in the nose, [17] . [260.] ► Painful itching above the root of the nose (after two hours and a half), [14] . ► Smell. ► He smells the medicine in the open air (after a quarter of an hour), [14] .
Nose feels stopped up; no air passes through. (Coryza with sore throat.).

face.text: FACE. ► Sleepy look (after three hours), [14] . ► Pale look (after seven days), [14] . ► Face yellow, earthy, [37] . ► Face stopped yellow, [37] . ► The liver spots in the same seem to become more distinct than ever (after one hour and a quarter), [14] . ► Puffy face, [25] . ► The features become spasmodically distorted, (soon), [26] . ► Cheeks. ► Burning internally in the right cheek (after one hour and a half), [14] . [270.] ► Tension as if one pressed firmly with a nail on the left zygoma, with some itching (after three hours), [14] . ► Chin. ► Inclination to close the teeth tightly, as in tetanus (after a few minutes), [38] . ► A pain in the articulation of the jaw as if it would be dislocated, [37] . ► A stitch near the left side of the chin, in the afternoon, [14] . ► Fine stitch in the left lower jaw, while walking in the open air (after three-quarters of an hour), [14] . ► Transient tearing in the left angle of the lower jaw, and the lower back teeth (after three-quarters of an hour), [14] . ► Violent bruised pain in the whole jaw, lasting half an hour, at 7 P.M., [14] . ► Gnawing as if in the bone, on the right side near the chin (after three-quarters of an hour), [14] .

(Sunken face with) countenance pale, sallow, and grey. Face wan, or bloated and swollen. Distortion of the features. Cramps in the jaws. Twitching in muscles of face. Eruption round mouth. Titillation in the face, as if flies and spiders were crawling over the skin. Lock-jaw. Painful sensations in the under jaw and lower teeth.

Face : yellow spots ; sunken, with livid grey yellow complexion ; blue, with gasping ; bloated ; idiotic. Twitching and convulsions of facial muscles. Titillation, as if flies and spiders were crawling over skin. Eruption around mouth. Lockjaw.

mouth.text: MOUTH. ► Teeth. ► A back tooth in the left lower row seems larger and longer (after one hour and a quarter), [14] . ► A painful sensation in the back teeth of the left side, relieved by biting the teeth together, [37] . [300.] ► Increased saliva in the mouth, with dryness of the throat, [38] . ► Accumulation of saliva in the mouth, and ecptysis (after two hours), [14] . ► Frequent spitting of saliva (after a quarter of an hour), [14] . ► Watery saliva collects in the mouth (after two hours), [14] . ► A constant thin watery saliva in the mouth, of a feverish taste, [37] . ► Collection of sourish saliva in the mouth (after ten minutes), [14] . ► Accumulation of tenacious mucus in the throat, every morning, when fasting, [38] . ► Accumulation of water in the mouth (after half an hour), [14] . ► Accumulation of water in the mouth, with nausea, lasting half an hour, [14] . ► Accumulation of water, qualmishness and nausea, disappearing for three-quarters of an hour, and again returning, relieved by eructations (after three-quarters of an hour), [14] . ► Taste. [310.] ► Taste flat, insipid, [37] . ► Feverish taste, all day, [27] . ► Taste slimy and pasty, [37] . ► Taste and odor of peach brandy in the mouth (after a quarter of an hour), [14] .

[330.] ► Scraping in the throat before and during cough (after three hours), [14] . ► Scraping in the throat and larynx, with hoarseness and frequent need to cough (after 16 drops), [12] . ► Rawness in the throat, lasting an hour, frequently returning in the afternoon (after half an hour), [14] . ► Rawness in the throat with hacking (after a quarter of an hour), [14] . ► Rawness in the throat, causing some cough, soon disappearing, in the morning (fourth day), [14] . ► Rawness and scraping in the throat, which provokes a hacking cough (after five minutes), [14] . ► Frequent hacking on account of rawness in the throat (after three-quarters of an hour), [14] . ► Speech is difficult on account of rawness in the throat (after three-quarters of an hour), [14] . ► Roughness of the throat (after 20 drops), [11] . ► Roughness and scraping in the throat, on swallowing, somewhat relieved after swallowing (after five hours), [14] . [340.] ► A choking pain in the upper part of the pharynx, which involves the chest and causes a kind of oppression in it; this pain then extends over the whole neck and face; with dull sticking pain in the teeth, and a feeling in some of them as if they had been raised up, [37] . ► She was able to swallow only after consciousness had somewhat returned, [16] . ► External Throat. ► Pain as from stiffness in the left side of the neck and throat, neither aggravated nor relieved by motion (after forty hours), [37] . ► Spasmodic contractive sensation in the anterior cervical muscles, while drinking (after one hour), [14] .

(second day), [13] . ► Eructation and Hiccough. ► Inclination to (ineffectual) eructations (after a quarter of an hour), [14] . ► Eructations and ineffectual sneezing (after a quarter of an hour), [14] . ► Frequent tasteless eructations (after 25 drops), [5] . ► Incomplete eructations (after one hour and a half), [14] . ► Empty eructations (after five minutes and two hours), [14] . ► Empty eructations, after breakfast, [14] . ► Frequent empty eructations (after one hour), [14] . ► Eructations of air (after three-quarters of an hour), [14] . [360.] ► Eructations tasting of the food, at 2 P.M., [14] . ► Eructations of the breakfast (after half an hour), [14] . ► Eructations twice, tasting of the porridge that had just been eaten (after one hour and a quarter), [14] . ► Eructations tasting of the drug, followed by burning in the stomach (after a quarter of an hour), [14] . ► Sweetish eructations (after half an hour), [14] . ► Bitter eructations, during and after breakfast (after one hour and a quarter), [14] . ► Eructations of a rancid taste (after five minutes), (and also on previous days), [14] . ► Eructations tasting of bitter almonds (after five minutes), [14] . ► Risings of tasteless water, after which the nausea gradually disappears (after half an hour), [14] . ► Heat rises from the stomach (after half an hour), [14] . [370.] ► A feeling as though hot vapor rose from the stomach to the shoulders (after one hour), [14] . ► Hiccough, twice (after a quarter of an hour); also, all day, [14] . ► Nausea and Vomiting. ► Nausea, [23] ; immediately, [14] . ► Nausea in the stomach (after one hour), [14] . ► Nausea and qualmishness in the stomach (after a quarter of an hour), [14] . ► Nausea and vomiting, [27] . ► Nausea, stomachache, after dinner (second day), [14] . ► Nausea, with colic, soon disappearing, in the morning, [37] . ► Slight transient nausea (after 20 drops), [10] . ► Became nauseated in the morning, with a feeling of hunger in the stomach, yet food nauseated him, [37] . [380.] ► A peculiar feeling in the epigastric region, not exactly nausea, but somewhat similar to the sensation that precedes vomiting (after 15 drops), [5] . ► He feels sick and nauseated (after ten hours), [37] . ► He became sick, as if he had taken an emetic, in the morning in bed (after twenty-four hours), [37] . ► He became faint and qualmish about the heart, with anxious warmth, as if he had taken an emetic, and could not vomit; in the afternoon (after forty hours), [37] . ► Vomiting, [17] , [23] . ► Stomach. ► Moving about in the stomach (after five minutes), [14] . ► Rumbling in the stomach, followed by sour eructations (after half an hour), [14] . ► Prolonged rumbling in the stomach, [14] . ► Agreeable warmth in the stomach and abdomen (soon), [14] . ► Feeling of emptiness in the stomach (after 35 drops), [5] . [390.] ► Feeling of weakness in the stomach (after one hour and a half), [14] . ► Discomfort in the stomach, as from fasting (after a quarter of an hour), [14] . ► Feeling as of a disordered stomach, after dinner, disappearing after eating bread, [14] . ► Pain in the epigastric region, while urinating, disappearing after urinating (after two hours), [14] . ► Pain in the stomach with qualmishness (after five to six hours), [13] . ► Pain and nausea in the stomach (after half an hour), [14] . ► Pain in the stomach similar to a faintness, in the morning (sixth day), [14] . ► Violent pain in the stomach, soon followed by loss of speech, and death after one hour; without vomiting, convulsions, discharges from the bowels, or any external alteration (from about 10 drachms of Aqua Laurocerasi), [28] . ► Most violent pain in the stomach, followed by death, [28] . [In a young person who had drank some Aq. Lauro, from a bottle.] ► Burning in the stomach, [14] . Lauro, in an hour.] ► Feeling of contraction in the epigastric region (after half an hour), [14] . ► Contractive feeling in the stomach, at 2 P.M., [14] . ► A cramplike pulling pain in the pit of the stomach, in the evening on getting into bed and lying on one side; with a small, weak, contracted pulse; this only disappeared when lying quietly on the back (after sixteen hours), [37] . ► Pressure in the stomach, with nausea (after half an hour), [14] . ► Severe pressure in the stomach, [22] . [410.] ► Violent pressure in the epigastric region, [39] . ► Cramplike sticking pain above the pit of the stomach, [37] . ► Violent stitch in the pit of the stomach (after two hours and a half), [14] . ► Violent stitch, extending through the pit of the stomach into the small of the back (after half an hour), [14] . >>>>> Copyright © Médi-T ® 2000 Laur.

rectum.text:

[510.] ► Fine sticking in the rectum, after urging to stool, [14] . ► A stitch as with an awl in the rectum during stool, which shoots from above downward; followed by discharge of some pasty fæces, [37] . ► Three tearings in succession in the rectum (after two hours), [14] . ► Crawling in the rectum as from worms, in the evening in bed, [14] . ► Itching and crawling in the rectum, in the morning (second day), [14] . ► Desire for stool with passage of only flatus, while walking (after one hour), [14] . ► Ineffectual desire for stool; she was obliged to go frequently, [14] .
STOOL. ► Diarrha. ► Diarrha once, followed by burning in the anus (seventh day), [14] . ► Diarrha, without tenesmus, in the evening (fifth day), [14] . ► Diarrha twice, with tenesmus, in the morning (fourth day), [14] . [520.] ► (The diarrha, with tenesmus, from which he had suffered ceased), (after two hours), [14] . ► Diarrha, with tenesmus, returns in the afternoon, [14] . ► Diarrha, at one time with, at another without, tenesmus (in the evening, first and second days, and in the morning, third day), [14] . ► Diarrha, consisting of thin greenish mucus, with contraction in the groin, four times in two hours, without relief; at 2 P.M. (second day), [14] . ► (This prover was tormented by an obstinate diarrha, commencing the day after the last dose, and lasting nine days; he had three watery stools daily, especially after midnight; which, however, were not ascribed to the action of the drug), [2] . ► Involuntary stool and micturition, [40] . ► Soft stool twice (first evening), [14] . ► Stool in the evening the third time, and after the stool tenesmus, [14] . ► Soft stool twice in succession, without relief of the pain, at 5 P.M., [14] . ► Two soft stools (second morning), [14] . [530.] ► Desire for stool; five minutes after a soft stool (after one hour and a half), [14] . ► Soft stool at 4 P.M., [14] . ► Urging and soft stool, after dinner, [14] . ► Pressure and soft stool, followed by some tenesmus (after one hour and a half), [14] . ► Soft stool, with pressing (after one hour), [14] . ► Thin stool (after two hours), [14] . ► Soft stool with tenesmus (second morning), [14] . ► Soft stool first (after eighteen hours); after the stool burning in the anus; during the stool twinging in the upper abdomen; after dinner, [14] . ► Stool pasty, seven hours earlier than usual, with slight pain as from a cold, about 5 A.M., [37] . ► Stool several hours later than usual, but of the normal consistence, [37] . (second day), [14] . ► Stool at first somewhat hard, afterwards somewhat softer, with colic, as from taking cold, and tenesmus in the rectum, as though more stool would follow, [37] . ► Hard stool, contrary to habit, [14] . ► Hard stool, at noon, with urging, [14] . ► Hard stool, with some pressure (after three-quarters of an hour), [14] . ► Stool hard, with pressure (second morning), [14] . ► Hard stool, with pressure, followed by burning in the anus (eighth day), [14] . ► Stool hard, without much pressure, twelve hours too late, [37] . urinary.text: URINARY ORGANS. ► Bladder. ► It was impossible to pass a drop of urine, the bladder and sphincter ani were completely paralyzed; this condition lasted five days, [17] . ► Pressure upon the bladder after dinner; he had to press a long time before the urine passed, and then but little was evacuated; but he felt very much relieved (after three days), [37] . ► Urethra. ► Burning in the urethra, and urging after urinating (second morning), [14] . ► Itching in the forepart of the urethra, [37] . ► Micturition. [560.] ► Micturition (after three minutes), (he had urinated an hour previously), [14] . ► Urinating a second time, contrary to habit (after one hour), [14] . ► Urinates the fourth time, at 4 P.M., [14] . ► Frequent emission of scanty urine, [14] . ► He is obliged to rise from bed early in the morning to urinate, but only a little passes, [37] . ► Only a few drops of urine during the stool, [14] . ► The urine passed slowly and indolently, [37] . ► The urine waited some time before it passed, in the morning (eighth day), [14] . ► Urine after three hours as usual, but she was obliged to wait for some time before it passed, preceded by painful urging, [14] . ► Urine. [570.] ► Urine increased (third, fourth, and fifth days), [14] . ► Urine increased in the morning (second day), [14] . ► The urine is apparently increased; the first since the morning after taking (after three hours and a half), [14] . ► The urine seems to be increased in the afternoon, and becomes turbid over night, [14] . ► Urine increased in the afternoon, soon depositing a thin cloud, [14] . ► Urine increased in the evening, with burning while urinating (first day), [14] . ► Urine increased while walking (at 4 P.M., and also second day), [14] . ► Urine a pale yellow, at 3 P.M., the second time; deposits a cloud, [14] . ► Scanty urine (after half an hour), [14] . ► Diminished secretion of urine; he urinates only three times in twenty-four hours, each time but little passes, [37] . [580.] ► No urine (first day), [14] . ► Urine pale and of the usual amount (after one hour), [14] . ► Urine pale, seems to be increased (after one hour), [14] . ► Urine yellow and scanty (after three hours and a half), [14] . ► Urine watery yellow, scanty (after three-quarters of an hour), [14] . ► Urine pale yellow, the second time since rising (after one hour), [14] . ► Urine golden yellow, after a short time a cloud forms in the middle of it, [37] . ► Urine too acrid, so that the pudenda are made sore thereby (at least, this seems to be the cause of the burning), [14] . ► Urine frothy all day; of the usual quantity (after two hours); three times in the day, [14] . ► The urine after standing a quarter of an hour looks like thick clayey water, [37] . [590.] ► A thin pellicle like a spider's web forms after a time on the clear urine; in the middle of the urine large flakes float, and at the bottom of the vessel there is a reddish sediment, [37] . ► The urine deposits a small cloud (after two hours), [14] . ► The urine is clear when passing, but after awhile deposits very much red sediment, with white flakes of a gelatinous consistence (after four hours), [37] . ► Urine deposits a thickish red sediment, [7] . SEXUAL ORGANS. ► Male. ► Erections in the evening and at night, [37] . ► Erections without fantasies, which had not happened for a long time, at 7 and 9 P.M., [14] . ► Voluptuous itching beneath the prepuce, with desire for coition, [37] . ► Female. ► Menstruation eight days too early (after six days), [14] . ► Menstruation eight days too early, though not more profuse than usual (after seven days), [14] . ► Menstruation eight days too early; during the first four days tearing in the vertex only at night, disappearing in the morning after rising; the discharge lasted eight days, was more profuse than usual, and painful; the blood was thin, [14] . [600.] ► Menstruation more profuse and long-lasting than usual (after seventeen days), [14] . ► Menstruation at the right time, but more profuse than usual; on the second day toothache; on the third day very violent cutting colic from 2 to 4 P.M., relieved by the application of warm clothes to the abdomen; on the fourth day menstruation ceased (after one day longer than usual; almost without difficulty), [14] .

Retention of urine. Pale yellow, watery urine. Frothy, acrid urine. Thick, reddish sediment in urine. Involuntary emission of urine. Paralysis of bladder. Pain in region of stomach during emission of urine.

Suppressed urine. θ Cholera. Retention of urine from paralysis of bladder, or urine is voided slowly. Involuntary urination. Urine : pale yellow ; thick, reddish or mahogany colored sediment, with floating jellylike flocks. Pain in region of stomach when urinating. Burning in urethra, and pressing after urinating. Itching in forepart of urethra. Acrid urine, corroding labia. Urinary difficulties, with palpitation of heart and gasping for breath, coming on by spells.

[610.] ► Scraping in the larynx, with hoarseness; followed by increased secretion of mucus in the larynx, and frequent provocation to cough (after 14 drops), [12] . ► Tickling in the larynx, [12] . ► Tickling and rawness in the trachea (after half an hour), [14] . ► Fine tickling in the trachea, during the rawness (after a quarter of an hour), [14] . ► Tickling in the trachea provokes cough (after half an hour), [14] . ► Crawling in the upper bronchi, as if tickled with a feather, [37] . ► Itching beneath the larynx, causing a hacking cough, [37] . ► Voice. ► Deep base voice (after three-quarters of an hour), [14] . ► Hoarseness, [12] ; (after 20 drops), [11] . ► Hoarseness and rawness in the throat, when swallowing and when not (after one hour and a half), [14] . [630.] ► A dry, short cough, with a feeling as if mucus were hanging in the throat and could not be loosened; an hour afterwards the mucus loosens easily, [37] . ► Short hacking cough, at 2 P.M., [14] . ► Short hacking cough, caused by tickling in the larynx (after one hour and a half), [14] . ► Hacking cough on account of rawness in the trachea (after one hour), [14] . ► Easy hacking of a piece of mucus (second morning), [14] . ► Respiration. ► Slow, moaning, rattling respiration, [23] . ► Sobbing respiration, [37] . ► Respiration very slow and scarcely perceptible, [18] . ► Frequent deep breathing (after one hour), [14] . ► On inspiration breathing was impeded in the epigastric region (after one hour and a quarter), [14] . [640.] ► Very short breath on account of sticking pain in the right shoulder and liver, [37] . ► Not so much dyspna as usual, while walking, but motion was easy (fourth day), [14] . >>>>> Copyright © Médi-T ® 2000 Laur.

Cyanosis and dyspnœa; worse, sitting up. Patient puts hands on heart. Cough, with valvular disease. Exercise causes pain around heart. Tickling, dry cough. Dyspnœa. Constriction of chest. Cough, with copious, jelly-like, or bloody expectoration. Small and feeble pulse. Threatening paralysis of lungs. Gasping for breath; clutches at heart

VOICE AND LARYNX. TRACHEA AND BRONCHIA. [25] [Laur.]. Scraping in larynx, with increased secretion of mucus ; hoarseness. Spasmodic constriction of trachea. Laryngismus stridulus ; heart affected. Breathing : slow, feeble, moaning or rattling ; almost imperceptible ; panting ; very difficult. Dyspnœa, with sensation as if lungs would not be sufficiently expanded, or as if pressed against spine. Spasmodic oppression of chest. Gasping, suffocating spells ; clutches at heart ; palpitation. Cough : short, titillating, nervous, from heart disease ; cannot lie down ; whizzing, with sensation as if mucous membranes were too dry ; < towards evening, from motion, stooping, eating and drinking, or warmth ; with copious, jelly-like sputa, dotted with bloody points. Dry, almost constant, titillating cough ; throat and mouth feel as if burnt. Cough, with whistling sound. Whooping cough, dry, whistling, no sputum ; impending paralysis of lungs. Cough, with evening aggravation, severe cramps in chest, and rapid sinking of vital forces. θ Whooping cough. Coughed continuously for several nights as soon as he laid down. θ Mitral stenosis.

[710.] ► Pulse 12 beats slower than usual (after 35 drops), [5] . ► Pulse 5 or 6 beats slower than usual (after 16 drops), [12] . ► Pulse 4 to 6 beats less than usual (after 14 drops), [12] . ► Pulse 4 or 5 beats slower (after 10 drops); 5 or 6 beats slower (after 15 drops); 8 to 10 beats slower (after 20 drops); 12 beats slower (after 30 drops), [8] . ► Pulse diminished from 3 to 4 beats (after 8 drops), [12] . ► Pulse sank from 70 to 65 (after 15 drops), [6] . ► Pulse sank to 65 (ten minutes after 10 drops); to 60 (ten minutes after 15 drops); the pulse also became slower after succeeding doses, [2] . ► Pulse sank from 70 to 64 (after 35 drops), [6] . ► Pulse small and slow, 59 to 63 (after 60 drops), [4] . ► Pulse 67 to 70, normal; small and slow, 60 to 63 (a quarter of an hour after 50 drops), [4] . [720.] ► Pulse, usually 70 and over, was only 60 in a minute after 10 drops, [1] . ► Pulse small, contracted, slow, 58 to 59 (a quarter of an hour after 112 drops), [4] . ► The pulse in the forearm and carotids, and also the beat of the heart, were scarcely perceptible; the beats followed each other at intervals of two seconds, [26] . ► Pulse small, [40] . ► The pulse was less changed after 25 drops than after a former small dose (10 drops), [1] .

back.text: NECK AND BACK. ► Neck. ► Stiffness in the nape of the neck, [37] . ► Tension in the right side of the neck, noticed on moving the head (after three hours), [14] . ► Pressure in the nape of the neck, in the open air, disappearing in the house (after one hour), [14] . ► Painful pressure in the nape of the neck, which compels her to hold the head forward; on raising it, a painful tension; at 6.30 P.M., lasting an hour, [14] . ► Cramplike pressive pain in the left cervical vertebræ on turning the head towards the left, not at all noticed when at rest, [37] . [730.] ► Fine sticking in the right side of the neck (after three hours and a half), [14] . ► A stitch in the nape of the neck, towards the left shoulder, at 2 o'clock, [14] . ► Tearing in the neck, then in the left shoulder; not disappearing on rubbing; after dinner, [14] . ► Violent beating, as with a hammer, in the right side of the neck, while running (after two hours), [14] . ► Back. ► Tension in the back and between the shoulders, that extends into the neck (after thirty-six hours), [37] . ► Slight sticking in the back (after half an hour), [14] . ► Stitches into the back (after one hour and a quarter), [14] . ► Dorsal. ► A burrowing and boring between the shoulders, little noticed during motion, but very much aggravated during rest, [37] . ► Pressure inward between the shoulders (after one hour), [14] . ► Pressive pain beneath the point of the right scapula, [37] . [740.] ► Sticking pain between the shoulders, [37] . ► A stitch between the scapulæ, on lacing the corsets (after three-quarters of an hour), [14] . ► Stitch in the right scapula, towards the axilla, while standing, at 3.30 P.M., [14] . ► Stitches in the tip of the left scapula, on inspiration (after one hour), [14] . ► Fine tearing on the upper part of the left scapula (after one hour and a half), [14] . ► Stitches and burning in the middle of the right scapula (after two hours), [14] . ► Two stitches in succession in the right scapula, towards the axilla (after one hour), [14] . ► Sharp stitch in the lower portion of the right scapula (after three-quarters of an hour, and frequently), [14] . ► Several fine stitches, in succession, in the middle of the right scapula (after one hour and a half), [14] . ► Lumbar. ► Pain in the small of the back, as from weariness; on becoming erect, frequently (after three-quarters of an hour), [14] . [750.] ► Stitches in the right side of the small of the back (after one quarter of an hour), [14] . ► *A feeling extending from the abdomen into the small of the back, as if something large had fallen down (after one hour and a half), [14] . ► Pain like a stiffness in the small of the back and back, when writing; it immediately disappears on becoming erect, soon returning, [37] . ► Sacral. ► Burning in the coccyx, at 4 P.M., [14] .

Painful stiffness in l. side of neck and nape. Compressed feeling in shoulders and nape, as well as in arms and back, with sudden palpitation which wakes her up at night. Pressure in nape esp. in open air, compelling him to bend head forward. Spinal irritation; pain down back every four hours, darting up to head and chest and causing a feeling of suffocation, with constant sick feeling and drowsiness; > from sleep. Sensation of a heavy lump falling from just above umbilicus to small of back. Painful stiffness in small of back, when writing; > immediately on becoming erect, but it soon returns. Stitches in r. side of small of back. Severe pain in sacral region extending to pubis. Burning in coccyx, 4 p.m.

Painful stiffness in left side of neck, nape and small of back. Pressure in nape of neck, particularly in open air, compelling him to bend head forward. Severe pain in sacral region, extending to pubis.

extremities.text: EXTREMITIES IN GENERAL. ► The limbs were very flexible, and remained wherever they were placed, [16] . ► The limbs seem paralyzed and lose their sensation, [22] . ► Extremities paralyzed and cold, but not insensible, [40] . ► Paralysis of the hands and feet, [49] . ► The limbs become quite numb and insensible, [39] . ► A feeling as if the joints were only loosely articulated, [37] . [760.] ► Paralytic-like pains in the articular extremities of several joints, [37] .
Toe and finger nails become knotty. Skin blue. Sprained pains in hips, thighs and heels. Cold, clammy feet and legs. Clubbing of fingers. Veins of hands distended

Pressure on right shoulder or in shoulder joint. Pains as from lameness, also stitches, in right shoulder. Pimples on right upper arm. Stitch in both elbows. Pain, as if sprained, in right wrist joint. Distension of veins on hands. θ Heart disease. Rough, scaly skin between fingers, with burning when touched by water. Pain, as if sprained, in left hip joint. Sticking in left knee. The feet go to sleep when crossing legs or sitting. Ulcerative pains in lower part of heels. Stiffness of feet after rising from a seat. Cold, clammy feet, up to knees. θ Chorea. θ Heart disease. Ends of fingers and toes enlarged, knob-like. θ Heart disease. Stinging and tearing in limbs. Painless paralysis of limbs.

upper_extremities.text: SUPERIOR EXTREMITIES. ► A feeling as though both arms were drawn inward, although she could stretch them out without pain (after three-quarters of an hour), [14] . ► Shoulder. ► Weariness in the shoulders, [13] . ► Paralytic pain in the right shoulder, [37] . ► The suspender over the right shoulder becomes very annoying, [37] . ► Painful pressure and tension in the ligaments of the left shoulder, [37] . ► A pressive pain on the top of the right shoulder, or a feeling as if he had received a blow a few days before, [37] . ► Pressive pain in the bones of the left shoulder, [37] . ► Violent pressive pain in the bones of the right shoulder (after thirty-six hours), [37] . ► Violent pressive pains in the right shoulder-joint woke him every half hour at night, and he tossed about the bed (after twenty-four hours), [37] . [770.] ► Rheumatic pressive pain in the right shoulder-joint, aggravated by bending the arm backward, [37] . ► Sticking pain in the right shoulder, [37] . ► Sticking and tearing in the left shoulder towards the forepart (after two hours), [14] . ► Very violent sticking pains in the right shoulder, [37] . ► Dull sticking, with tension, on the anterior surface of the left shoulder-joint, as if the tendons were too short; if she stretches the shoulder forward; not felt on any other motion of the arm (second day), [14] . ► Fine sticking in the right shoulder, at 2 P.M., [14] . ► A violent piercing pain in the right shoulder-joint woke him in the evening, only momentarily relieved by laying the arm over the head, [37] . ► Stitch beneath the left shoulder towards the forepart (after one hour), [14] . ► Stitches beneath the right axilla (after four hours), afterwards extending forward into the chest; with a joyous mood (after three hours), [14] . ► Violent stitch in the left shoulder (after two hours), [14] . [780.] ► Fine stitch in front of the right shoulder, together with a stitch in the axilla (after a quarter of an hour), [14] . ► A fine, but very painful stitch in the right axilla at 2.30 P.M., [14] . ► Tearing in the right shoulder (after one hour), [14] . ► Pain, almost like a tearing, in the left shoulder, not disappearing on rubbing (after three-quarters of an hour), [14] . ► Violent tearing in the left shoulder, disappearing on rubbing (after three hours and a half), [14] . ► Violent, painful tearing in the left shoulder from 6 to 8.30 P.M., [14] . ► Violent but transient tearing in the right shoulder (after one hour and a quarter), [14] . ► Arm. ► The head of the right humerus is painful, as if it were swollen and had not room enough in the capsule of the joint, [37] . ► Tension on the inner surface of the upper arm, by the elbow, as if the skin were raised by a needle; at 2.30 P.M., [14] . ► Pinching pain on the anterior surface of the right upper arm, [37] . [790.] ► Pressive pain in the tendon of the deltoid muscle, [37] . ► A stitch in the right upper arm, followed by bruised pain, extending into the shoulder (after half an hour), [14] . ► Three burning stitches in the right upper arm by the elbow (after half an hour), [14] . ► Tearing on the inner surface of the upper arm (after half an hour), [14] . ► Tearing in the middle of both upper arms, as if in the bone, not disappearing on rubbing (after half an hour), [14] . ► Sudden violent tearing in the muscles of the right upper arm (after one hour and a half), [14] . ► Gnawing in the right upper arm, relieved while pressing upon it, afterwards sensitive to pressure (after one hour), [14] . ► Elbow. ► Pain in the left elbow, [13] . ► Sticking in the left elbow (after one hour and a half), [14] . ► Stitch in the lower surface of the right elbow (after two hours), [14] . A.] ► A jerking pain in the left thigh just above the knee, [37] . ► Knee. ► Trembling in the knees when moving about in the open air, disappearing when sitting (after half an hour), [14] . [See S. 934.] ► A small pimple between the chin and lower lip, painful only on touch (after one hour), [14] . ► Itching of the pimples between the chin and lips, disappearing on scratching, at 2 P.M., [14] . ► Burning of the pimples between the lips and chin, at 3 P.M., [14] . ► The pimples on the lips itch violently, disappearing after scratching (after 4 hours), [14] . [960.] ► Itching on the right frontal eminence (after one hour), [14] . ► Itching on the right frontal eminence, and burning after scratching (after half an hour), [14] . ► Itching on the left frontal eminence, disappearing after scratching (after three-quarters of an hour), [14] . ► Itching in the left eyebrow (after two hours), [14] . ► Itching in the left upper eyebrow, relieved by scratching (after three-quarters of an hour), [14] . ► Itching in the left lobule, not disappearing on scratching (after one hour and a half), [14] . ► Itching of the nose, especially of the tip (after five hours), [14] . ► Itching in the right side of the nose, so that she was obliged to scratch it till it bled, in the morning (fourth day), [14] . ► Itching in the left nostril, not relieved by rubbing (after two hours), [14] . ► Itching in the middle of the upper lip, disappearing after scratching (after half an hour), [14] . [970.] ► Itching between the chin and lips of the right side, not relieved by scratching (after two hours), [14] . ► Itching in the right groin, disappearing on scratching (after two hours), [14] . ► Itching between the shoulders, at 3 P.M., [14] . ► Itching and crawling, as from fleas, between the shoulders (after half an hour), [14] . ► Itching behind the right shoulder, not relieved by scratching (after half an hour), [14] . ► Itching on the back, near the right scapula (after one hour), [14] . ► Itching on the coccyx, that disappears on rubbing, [37] . ► Itching on the right upper arm, after scratching pimples which burn (fourth day), [14] . ► Itching in the bend of the left elbow, burning after scratching (after half an hour), [14] . ► Itching on the external surface of the left elbow, disappearing after scratching (after half an hour), [14] . sleep.text: SLEEP AND DREAMS. ► Sleepiness. ► Yawning and sleepiness, [14] . ► Yawning, without sleepiness, [14] . ► Frequent yawning (after a quarter of an hour, and two hours, also second afternoon), [14] . ► Frequent yawning, without sleepiness (after a quarter of an hour), [14] . [990.] ► Frequent yawning, with chilliness, [37] . ► Frequent yawning, with shivering (after three hours and a half), [14] . ► Frequent yawning and shivering, followed by gooseflesh, lasting two minutes (after half an hour), [14] . ► Very frequent yawning, [37] . ► Sleepiness, [14] ; (after 20 drops), [5] . ► Sleepiness, immediately after dinner, [14] . ► Sleepiness very early, at 6 P.M.; he cannot keep sleep away, [14] . ► Sleepiness, yawning, and stretching (after one hour), [14] . ► Great sleepiness in the evening; in the morning it was difficult to rise (after 14 drops), [12] . ► Great sleepiness, with burning in the eyes, in the evening, [14] . [1000.] ► Inclination to sleep (after 20 drops), [10] . ► Inclination to sleep as early as 11 A.M., so that he was obliged to lie on the lounge and sleep; after sleeping a quarter of an hour, he found is almost impossible to rise and move about on account of weakness (after 20 drops), [11] . ► Very sleepy, weak (after two hours and a half), [14] . ► So sleepy that he could not keep from falling asleep while in the house, and it was only out of doors that he was able to keep awake (after 20 drops), [5] . ► Sleep irresistible after dinner, after which he felt very well (after 10 drops), [5] . ► He was unable to keep from sleeping in the evening; was awakened about 7 o'clock, for supper; he sat up and ate, but immediately fell asleep again without speaking; after awhile he started in sleep, then jumped high up, frightening those about him, stood up and walked to the middle of the room, with staring eyes and red face; at last he lay down without speaking and slept soundly till morning (first evening), [14] . ► Sleep from noon till 3 P.M. (quite contrary to habit), though he had slept well the night previous; and he also slept well on the succeeding night, [5] . ► Sleep sound and longer than usual, [14] . ► Very sound sleep at night continuing late in the morning; with some perspiration, [38] . ► Sleeplessness. ► Very difficult falling asleep, [13] . [1010.] ► Constant tossing about in bed; she cannot sleep before 1 A.M., [14] . ► Unable to sleep before 11 P.M., [14] . ► He awoke about midnight and could not fall asleep; was constantly obliged to toss about as if the bed were too hard, [14] . ► Uneasy sleep, frequent waking, [13] . ► Sleep prevented by unusual excitement and flushes of heat, [17] . ► Dreams. ► Sleep sound; full of lively and sad dreams, [14] . ► Very vivid though unremembered dreams, [14] . ► Confused dreams all night, [14] . ► A dream of which he was perfectly conscious in the night could not be recalled in the morning, [37] . ► Heavy, anxious dreams, [13] . [1020.] ► He dreamed that he was standing upon a very high and not very secure ledge, or upon a high scaffolding, without being anxious, [37] . ► Frightful dreams of fires, which wake her, [14] . ► Frightful dreams of dead men, or fires, [14] .
Spells of deep sleep, with snoring and stertorous breathing

Frequent yawning, often accompanied by shuddering, with cutis anserina. Insurmountable inclination to sleep during day (esp. after dinner) and early in evening. Deep snoring sleep. Soporous condition. Somnolence, sometimes like coma vigil. Sleeplessness from over-excitement, and sudden heat. Troublesome and agitated dreams.
Irresistible sleepiness, especially after dinner and in evening. Soporous condition ; deep, snoring sleep.

(second day), [14] . ► Chilliness in the afternoon; shivering through the skin several times, [37] . ► Chilliness after rising, about midnight, so that she was unable to get warm in bed for a long time, [14] . ► Chilliness for two hours, with a flat taste in the mouth, [37] . (second day), [14] . ► Coldness in the open air, disappearing in the room (after two hours), [14] . ► Coldness and shaking chill, in the house, not relieved by the warmth of the stove; lasting several minutes, without subsequent heat or thirst, in the evening, after walking in the open air, [14] . ► Internal coldness, [39] . ► An internal coldness extending over the whole body, [22] . ► Great coldness, from 6.30 to 8.30 P.M., without subsequent heat or thirst, [14] . ► Feeling of coldness in the open air, at 2 P.M., [14] . ► Feeling of coldness in a warm room; the nose is icy cold, at 3 P.M., [14] . (sixth evening), [14] . ► Shivering, with gooseflesh (after five minutes); on going out of doors (after two hours and a half), [14] . ► Agreeable coolness in the head, after the perspiration (after a quarter of an hour), [14] . ► Coldness of the feet, and much thirst, at 7 P.M., [14] . ► Coldness on the sole of the left foot, soon disappearing (after two hours), [14] . Hoarseness, roughness, and scraping in throat and pharynx. Deep bass voice. Spasmodic constriction of the trachea. Little short cough, excited by a tickling and scraping in throat. Abundant gelatinous expectoration, with small specks of blood. (Persistent cough in phthisis trachealis.). Acute drawings and shootings in shoulders and in arms. Pressure on r. shoulder or in the joint. In r. shoulder, pains as from lameness and stitches. Stitch in both elbows. Pain, as if sprained, in r. wrist-joint. Burning sensation in hands, with swelling of the veins. Trembling of hands. Skin dry and rough between the fingers, with burning when touched with water. Acute drawings and shootings in knees. Pain, as if sprained in l. hip-joint. Sticking in l. knee. The feet go to sleep (when crossing legs or sitting). Ulcerated pains in lower part of heels. Stiffness of feet after rising from a seat. Numbness of feet. Weak, anæmic, emaciated girls. Weak, apathetic, lies in bed in morning. Suffocation, sickness, drowsiness. Convulsive and spasmodic jerks, by fits. Internal inflammation. Trembling, esp. of hands and feet, during exercise in open air. Sudden weakness, with excessive nervous dejection. (Apoplexy, and paralysis of limbs.). Painless paralysis of the limbs. Fainting fits. Drawing and tearing pains in limbs. Pinching with sensation of tearing. Coldness of inner parts; heat of single part; internal chilliness and external heat. Want of vital energy, and of reaction. Sense of fatigue in whole body. Epileptic convulsions, with foaming at mouth, which is tightly closed. Tetanus. Painlessness of the ailments. Pulse small. Skin turns blue; toe- and finger-nails become knobby. Symptoms in evening; > at night, and in open air. In general > from sleep.

Want of energy of vital powers and want of reaction, especially in chest and heart affections. Rapid sinking of forces : long lasting fainting spells. Clonic spasms of limbs, with paralytic weakness ; no loss of consciousness. θ Typhoid. Much gasping for breath before, during, or after spasm, with bluish tint of skin ; after fright ; emaciation. θ Convulsions. Emotional chorea, fearful contortions and jactitations when awake ; restless sleep ; gasping for breath. Want of nervous reaction ; the well chosen remedy does not act. Chorea, with constant jerks, cannot keep still ; speech indistinct, gets angry when not understood ; gasping ; emaciation ; after fright. On day of burial of mother, ten years ago, sudden loss of consciousness, from which he did not recover for a long time ; since then, attacks more frequent and severe, finally, one or two a week, one lasting as long as nine hours ; before attack, paleness of face ; dropping of lower jaw ; sinking of arms to side ; wide open, staring eyes ; loss of consciousness ; respiration suspended ; pulse weak, scarcely perceptible ; face pale ; limbs stiff ; hands clenched, thumbs not drawn in ; eyes closed ; mouth slightly open, no foaming ; in beginning of attack grinding teeth ; after one half hour to nine hours changes position of limbs and stretches, then, between long intervals, takes deep breaths, opens eyes, trembles all over and complains of great lassitude and severe headache. Epilepsy ; tetanus. Apoplexy, with paralysis. After feeling ill eight days, during which time several attacks of vomiting of tasteless water occurred, paroxysm sets in ; great anxiety, eyes turned up and fixed upon one spot, pupils dilated, vanishing of sight, roaring and ringing in ears, trembling and twitching of muscles of face, distortion of face, rush of blood to head, heat and redness of face, chattering teeth, first trembling of upper then of lower limbs, followed by shaking of body as in a severe chill, but with heat and sweat of body ; twitching and spasmodic distortion of whole body and extremities ; palpitation ; moaning and groaning ; partial loss of consciousness ; paroxysms last several minutes ; during interval, confusion of head, pain in epigastrium, great lassitude, debility, anxiety and restlessness ; occasional short slumber disturbed by dreams of fire, startled awaking, or twitching of whole body ; occasionally delirium, cries for help ; no fever. One or two a week : attacks of loss of consciousness. From evening till midnight : heat after chill. Right : pressure of shoulder ; in shoulder joint ; pains as from lameness in shoulder ; pimples on upper arm ; pain as if sprained in wrist joint. Left : painful stiffness of side of neck ; pain as if sprained in hip joint ; sticking in knee. Coolness of forehead as from a draft of air ; as of a weight on top of head ; as if brain was loose and falling into forehead when stooping ; as of a veil before eyes ; nose as if stopped up ; as if flies and spiders were crawling over skin ; tongue as if burnt ; as if abscess in region of liver would burst ; as of a heavy lump falling from just above umbilicus to small of back ; as if lungs would not be sufficiently expanded ; as if lungs pressed against spine ; as if mucous membranes were too dry ; throat and mouth as if burnt ; pain as from lameness in r. shoulder ; pain as if sprained in right wrist joint. Pain : in hypochondrium ; as from subcutaneous ulceration in region of liver ; in region of stomach when urinating ; in external parts of thorax ; as if sprained in wrist joint ; as from lameness ; in epigastrium. Painfulness : of brain. Violent pain : in stomach. Severe pain : in head ; in sacral region to pubis ; in pleura. Tearing pains : in vertex ; in limbs. Cutting : in abdomen. Lacerating pain : in head. Pinching : about umbilicus. Stitches : in head ; in region of heart ; in precordial region ; in right shoulder ; in both elbows. Sticking pains : in liver ; in left knee. Stinging : in and below mamma ; in limbs. Beating, fluttering sensation : about heart. Beating : in head. Boring pain : in head. Stupefying pain : in whole head. Ulcerative pain : in lower part of heels. Dragging pain : in head. Colicky pains : in abdomen. Spasms : of chest. Spasmodic pains : in cardiac region. Aching pain : under frontal bone. Burning : in stomach and abdomen ; in urethra ; in and below mamma ; in chest ; between fingers. Painful stiffness : in left side of neck, nape and small of back. Pulsation : in head, with heat or coldness. Spasmodic constriction : of trachea. Oppression : on top of head ; of chest. Pressure : in nape of neck ; in right shoulder. Constriction : of throat. Contractive feeling : in region of stomach. Stiffness : of feet. Scraping : in larynx. Tingling : in ears. Contraction : of brain. Dryness : of mouth. Coldness : in forehead and vertex ; of extremities ; of entire body ; of tongue. Itching : of hairy scalp ; in ears ; in forepart of urethra. Child, æt. 8 months, sick some time ; cholera infantum. Two boys, æt. between 10 and 12 ; clonic convulsions during typhoid. Girl, æt. 12, very nervous, continually twitches muscles of face, after fright ; chorea. Boy, æt. 13, had rheumatism ; disease of heart. Three women, æt. 20 ; clonic convulsions during typhoid. Woman, suffering five years ; spasmodic pains about heart. Man, æt. 36, medium height, suffering appearance, compelled to do hard work ; attacks of loss of consciousness. Man, psoric taint, suffering from cramps, after great fright ; spasms. Woman, æt. 50 ; palpitation of heart. Woman, æt. 59 ; unmarried, one year ago had apoplexy cured by Hyosc. and Bellad. ; threatened apoplexy.

keynotes.text: color="#800000">LAUROCERASUS Cherry-laurel Spasmodic tickling cough, especially in cardiac patients, is often magically influenced by this drug. Lack of reaction, especially in chest and heart affections. Drink rolls audibly through œsophagus and intestines. General coldness, not ameliorated by warmth. Violent pain in stomach with loss of speech. Spasm of facial muscles and œsophagus. Asphyxia neonatorum.

Laurocerasus, the so-called "Laurel" of our gardens, is not a member of the Lauraceæ, though the Bay Laurel, Laurus nobilis, is. The Aqua Laurocerasi, prepared by distilling the fresh leaves, contains Hydrocyanic acid and is supposed to owe all its medicinal virtue to this fact. Milne says it is "used in spasmodic cough, and in phthisis; but it is better to employ the prussic acid itself." In the poisoning cases that have occurred the symptoms have been practically identical with those of persons poisoned with prussic acid; but the provings bring out more delicate shades of action and fully entitle the remedy to an individual place of its own. Coldness, blueness, epileptiform convulsions are common to both. Both have a dry, tickling cough; but Lauro. has also a cough with jelly-like expectoration dotted with bloody points. Lauro. has cyanosis both of the new-born infant and of heart disease. A peculiar "gasping" is indicative here─gasping without really breathing. In addition to the blueness there is twitching of the muscles of the face (which is also an indication for Lauro. in chorea). Clubbing of fingers, which is a common feature in cyanosis and phthisis, is a characteristic of Lauro. "Lack of reactive power," low vitality, is another keynote of Lauro. This is particularly so when occurring in chest affections. Long-lasting faints (Camph. has sudden fainting); seems to have no reactive power; face pale, blue; surface cold. Prostrate before getting up in morning and difficulty in opening eyes; attacks of indigestion and pains across lower abdomen, which come suddenly in the morning and generally disappear on getting up. Gnawing pain in lower abdomen going on for years in old men with occasional looseness of stool. If fluids are forced down the throat they roll audibly into stomach. When indicated in eruptive fevers the eruption is livid, after pressure with the finger the skin is long in regaining its colour. There are suffocative spells about the heart by sitting up; the patient is compelled to lie down (as with Psor.); though some heart symptoms have the opposite condition and compel the patient to sit up. Guernsey gives these leading symptoms: "Gasping for breath; the patient puts his hand to the heart as if there was some trouble there; this may result from running a short distance, which puts him completely out of breath; going upstairs, walking, or any exercise may bring the gasping on." Coldness is a common sensation, internal coldness and external heat. Cold tongue. Heat of single parts. Warmth on centre of forehead, then a coldness as from a draught of air lasting a long time. The left chest is most affected. There is before eating. Constricted sensations─in gullet; in rectum. There are some very noteworthy uterine symptoms, of menorrhagia and dysmenorrhœa, in the provings, and the value of them has been emphasised by cases related by Cooper in his work on Cancer and Cancer Symptoms (2nd ed., p. 60). The leading indications according to Cooper are: "A sense of fatigue pervades the whole system, with a very painful condition of the hard and indurated tissue of the parts affected; pain much > by sleep; tendency to oozing of blood, which is generally bright and mixed with gelatinous clots. This applies to chest as well as to uterine and rectal symptoms; only, the blood comes painlessly into the mouth, but with great pain Per vaginam. In most cases it will be found that the pains it relieves are such as start from the lower part of the spine and extend either round the pelvis or up to the head, and are accompanied with a sense of suffocation and a sick feeling, with drowsiness and a great desire to sleep that generally brings relief. In cases that are sleepless the desire for sleep is very great. Digestion is weak; bowels confined patient, low-spirited with flatulence and burning in chest after food constant tired, sick feeling; entire frame enfeebled; inclined to loss of flesh and hæmorrhages that are small in quantity and bright in colour. The flatus is audible and gurgling and rolls about the upper abdomen (p. 64)." In nervous affections "constant jerks; cannot keep still" and the characteristic "gasping" are leading indications. Peculiar sensations are: Coolness of forehead as from draught of air. Weight on top of head. As if brain loose and falling into forehead when stooping. As if a heavy lump falling from abdomen to small of back, as if a veil before eyes. As if nose stopped. As if flies and spiders crawling over skin. As if tongue, mouth, and throat were burnt. As if abscess in region of liver would burst. As if lungs could not be sufficiently expanded. As if lungs pressed against spine. As if mucous membranes were dry. Stitches are very prominent; also stiffness and pressure, especially pressure outward. Allen's indications are: An extremely nervous, excitable condition accompanying ailments. Diarrhœa as of green mucus, with suffocative spells about heart. Dry, harassing cough of phthisis. Spasmodic cough of later stages of whooping-cough when patient is much prostrated and has nervous spasmodic symptoms. Cough with valvular heart disease; cough incessant, especially on lying down; fluttering in heart and gasping, with cough. Alternation of chill, fever, and sweat in phthisis. E. Wigg (H. P., xii. 30) relates the case of May S., 7, who had been troubled some time with an almost incessant cough, for which many remedies were given without benefit. At last Wigg came to the conclusion that Lauro. was the remedy, and put ten drops of the 200th into five tablespoonfuls of water, ordering a teaspoonful of this to be taken every two hours when the child was awake. This was at 4 p.m. After the third dose she fell asleep. At 3 a.m. she awoke in a very excited condition. Her mother asked her if she had not been dreaming, but her tongue was so stiff she could not answer. Suddenly she began to tremble all over as if in a chill. After ten minutes she began to twitch and jerk. Wigg was sent for and found her in this convulsed state. She could not articulate for the thickness and heaviness of the tongue. The mind was clear. Recognising the action of Lauro. Wigg antidoted it with Camphor and later a cup of coffee, and she came all right in a few hours. She had no more of the cough. Many symptoms are > sitting up. Coughs continuously when lying down. Bending head forward > pressure in nape. Compelled to bend forward by contractive pain in groins. On the other hand suffocating spells about heart compel him to lie down. When she attempts to rise from recumbent posture, sensation of heavy lump falling from pit of stomach to back. Stooping . Sitting gasping; = feet to go to sleep. Crossing legs = feet to go to sleep. Motion cough; = gasping. After dinner: Hunger; irresistible drowsiness. Evening and night. Coldness in forehead and vertex is > in open air; vertigo, and pressure in nape are . External warmth does not > coldness. Approaching stove = nausea; cold. Being touched by water = burning between fingers.

A Text-Book of Materia Medica. (Characteristic, Analytical, and Comparative.) By Pr. Allen Corson Cowperthwaite, M. D., PH. D., LL. D. LAUROCERASUS . (Prunus Laurocerasus.) Natural order.- Rosaceae. Common name.- Cherry Laurel. General Analysis.- Acts upon the brain and spinal cord, paralyzing the nerve centers in a manner resembling the poison of Hydrocyanic acid. Characteristic symptoms. Mind.- (Laurocerasus) Insensibility and complete loss of sensation. (Op.) Head.- (Laurocerasus) Stupefaction, with vertigo. Stupefying pain in the whole head. Sensation of coldness in forehead (Arn.) and vertex (Calc. c., Phos.) as if a cold wind were blowing on it, descending through the neck to the back. Eyes.- (Laurocerasus) Obscuration of vision, as if a veil were before the eyes. (Caust., Hyos., Phos., Merc., Petrol., Sulph .) Eyes open and staring ; distorted. ( Bell ., Hyos., Stram .) Face.- (Laurocerasus) Sunken face, with livid, gray-yellow complexion. Twitching and convulsions of the facial muscles. (Cic.) Mouth.- (Laurocerasus) Lockjaw. ( Bell ., Cic., Hyos., Ign., Nux v ., Oenan.) Foam at mouth. (Cic., Coccul., Cupr., Hyos., Naja.) Loss of speech. (Dulc., Gels., Caust., Naja.) Throat.- (Laurocerasus) Impeded deglutition. (Bell., Hyos., Stram .) Spasmodic contraction in the throat and oesophagus. ( Bell., Hyos., Stram .) Stomach.- (Laurocerasus) Vomiting of food, with cough. Abdomen.- (Laurocerasus) Sensation like the falling of a heavy lump from just above the umbilicus to the small of back ; produced by talking or over exertion. Respiratory Organs.- (Laurocerasus) Spasmodic oppression of the chest. (Ign.) Dyspnoea, with sensation as if the lungs would not be sufficiently expanded. ( Asaf., Crot. tig .) Gasping for breath ; suffocating spells. Cough, with a whistling sound, as if the membranes were too dry. Stitches in the region of the heart. ( Kalm., Kali carb .) Heart and Pulse.- (Laurocerasus) Irregular beating of the heart, with slow pulse. (Digit., Lachn.) Generalities.- (Laurocerasus) Want of energy of the vital powers, and want of reaction. (Caps., Carb. v.) Painlessness with the ailments. Rapid sinking of the forces. (Ars., Camph., Sec. c.) Sleep.- (Laurocerasus) Deep, snoring sleep ; soporous condition. (Ars., Op ., Sulph.) Therapeutic Range.- (Laurocerasus) Apoplexia ; paralysis of special senses ; of organs of speech ; of lungs ; of extremities ; choreic convulsions ; clonic spasms ; epilepsy. Compare.- (Laurocerasus) Baryt. c., Bry., Calc. c., Hydroc. ac., Lyc., Nux v., Bell ., Op., Phos ., Puls., Rhus tox., Sep., Sulph., Verat. alb. Antidotes.- (Laurocerasus) Camph ., Coff ., Ipec., Op . Too large doses : Amm., strong coffee, cold effusions. Copyright © Robert Séror 2003

The many strange constitutional symptoms indicate feeble circulation and weak heart.

Great general coldness, that is not ameliorated by external warmth. It is like wrapping up a dead man. Yet if he approaches a warm stove nausea comes on. If he is in a warm room the sweat breaks out on the forehead and the forehead is cold; but if he moves about slowly in the open air the sweat ceases and the forehead warms up.

Want of animal heat. Want of reaction. Remedies act only as palliatives, act only as short acting remedies in constitutional disease, or the symptoms partially subside but the patient does not react. The patient does not convalesce.

It often cures the heart failure due to Digitalis in Old School hands, or when convalescence is attended by weak heart if there is cold skin and external heat is objectionable. It should be compared with Camph., Am. c. and Secale.

Suffocative spells about the heart, lying down; cardiac cough; gasping, twitching and jerks.
Lack of re-active power, low vitality, with blueness or cyanoses, especially in heart troubles.
Drink rolls audibly down through the œsophagus and intestines; very low pulse.

"Want of energy, of vital power; want of reaction, especially in chest and heart affections." And there is another characteristic symptom under this remedy in heart troubles: "The cyanosis, dyspnœa, etc., are worse when sitting up." Only one other remedy has this aggravation, and that is Psorinum. "Want of nervous reaction; the well-chosen remedy does not act."
"Capsicum, lack of reaction in persons of lax fibre."
Opium, in patients where there is no pain; stupidity and drowsiness.
Valerian and Ambra in nervous affections, well chosen remedies fail.
Carbo vegetabilis, collapse, coldness of knees, breath; perfect indifference.
Sulphur and Psorinum, where Psora complicates and hinders reaction.
Each one of these remedies may he called for in defective reaction, and there may be many more, and in each case, as with all remedies elsewhere, symptoms must decide which one.

mental_emotional.text: Mental dejection. Great anxiety, apprehension, and agitation, which do not allow a moment's rest, nor yet sleep in evening, but which disappear in open air. Repugnance to intellectual labour. Mental promptitude and precipitation. Inability to collect one's ideas. Weakness of memory. Fear and anxiety about imaginary evils. Intellectual incapacity. Mental dulness (insensibility) and loss of consciousness, with loss of speech and motion. She becomes irritable, talks too much; and then pains in shoulders and arms to tips of fingers come on, and she loses the power to hold things in her hands. Intoxication.

Loss of consciousness, with loss of speech and motion. Insensibility ; complete loss of sensation. Dulness of senses. Weakness of mind and loss of memory. Inability to collect one's ideas. Fear and anxiety about imaginary evils ; despondency. Vertigo : with stupefaction ; with disposition to sleep ; < in open air. Sudden bloating of face, vertigo, distortion of mouth, twitching of muscles of face ; loss of speech, with perfect consciousness. θ Threatened apoplexy. Coma, looks more like a deep, quiet sleep. θ Apoplexy. Apoplexy, with paralysis.

eyes.text: Burning pain in eyes. Dryness of eyes. Eyes widely open, or half-closed, convulsed, prominent, and fixed. Pupils dilated, and immovable. Darkness before the eyes; obscuration of sight. Eyes distorted. All objects appear larger than they really are.
Obscuration of vision ; sensation of a veil before eyes. Objects appear larger. Eyes : staring, wide open ; lightly closed ; distorted. Pupils dilated, immovable.

ears.text: Hardness of hearing. Tingling in ears. Itching in ears.
Hardness of hearing. Tingling in ears ; itching.

mouth_throat.text: Mouth dry and clammy. Froth at the mouth. Sensation of coldness on tongue. Loss of speech. Dry and rough tongue. White and dry tongue. Tongue feels cold, or burnt and numb. Swelling and stiffness of l. side of tongue. Sore throat with painful sensation, as if it were drawn downwards, during deglutition and at other times. Cramps in pharynx and œsophagus. Audible gulping when drinking. Deglutition is hindered or obstructed. Contraction of œsophagus when drinking. The drink he takes rolls audibly through œsophagus and intestines. Dull sticking pain as from a lump in throat, which extends to l. side of back. Sensation of swelling in pharynx. Sensation of coldness, or heat and burning pain in throat, and in bottom of palate.

Tongue : dry, rough ; dry and white ; cold or numb, as if burnt ; left side stiff and swollen, with loss of speech. Foam at mouth. θ Epilepsy. Dryness in mouth. Spasmodic deglutition. θ Chorea. Spasmodic contraction in throat and œsophagus. Drink rolls audibly through œsophagus and intestines.

stomach_abdomen.text: Sweetish, or acrid and irritating taste. Ardent thirst, with dryness of mouth. Entire loss of appetite, with clean tongue. Want of appetite, with repugnance to all food. Excessive craving. Sinking between meals, husky voice, brings up phlegm streaked with blood. Violent risings, either empty, or having taste of food. Excessive disgust, nausea, and vomiting, even of food. Hiccough. Bitter eructations. Violent pain in stomach, with loss of speech. Burning in stomach and abdomen, or coldness. Contractive pain in region of stomach, and cutting pain in abdomen. Vomiting of black matter. Aching of stomach. Pains in stomach, sufficiently violent to cause fainting. Great anguish in precordial region. Sensation of coldness, or burning in stomach, and in epigastrium. (Inflammation of stomach.). Weak digestion with confined bowels. Sticking pains in liver with pressure. Distension of region of liver, with pains, as from subcutaneous ulceration. Induration of liver. Atrophic nutmeg liver. Colic below navel, with incisive pain in intestines. Sensation of swelling, of size of a walnut, in l. side of abdomen. Cuttings, cramps, and contractions in abdomen. Sensation of coldness, or heat and burning in abdomen. (Inflammation of intestines.). Pains in l. intestine. Flatulent colic. Borborygmi, rumbling, and grumbling in abdomen and in stomach. Pinching in umbilical region. Flatulence pressure outward at perinæum; pressing on bladder. Pressure and tension beneath both Poupart's ligaments as if a part would press through. Contractive pains in groins; compelled to bend up for two hours (4 p.m.). Sharp stitches in groins.

Coldness and audible rumbling in whole abdomen.

stool_rectum.text: Constipation. Hard and tenacious evacuations, with straining. Ineffectual urging to stool, with emission of wind only. Diarrhœa, with tenesmus, incisive and burning pains in anus. Diarrhœa of green, slimy matter, with contraction in groins. Involuntary stools. Paralysis of sphincter ani. Cramp in rectum extends upwards from anus. Fine sticking in rectum after urging to stool. A stitch as with an awl in rectum during stool, which shoots from above downwards, and is followed by discharge of some pasty fæces. Three tearings in succession in rectum. Constriction of the rectum. Itching or crawling in rectum as from worms. No stool or urine is discharged.

Diarrheic stools : with tenesmus ; of green, liquid mucus, with suffocative spells about heart, forcing her to lie down ; involuntary. Green, watery stools ; drinks roll audibly through œsophagus and intestines ; suppression or retention of urine ; dilated pupils ; slow, feeble breathing ; irregular, imperceptible pupils ; slow, feeble breathing ; irregular, imperceptible pulse. θ Cholera infantum. Frequent, greenish, mucous stools ; entire loss of appetite, but thirsty ; child would not take anything but water, which could be heard gurgling down œsophagus to stomach ; respiration very slow and weak ; pulse almost imperceptible ; extremities cold as death, with coldness of entire body. θ Cholera infantum. Absence of vomiting and stools ; asphyxia ; coldness of body, pulselessness ; cloudiness of brain, fainting ; tetanic spasms ; suppression or retention of urine ; sensation of constriction in throat when swallowing. θ Cholera. Complete paralysis of sphincter ani.

male.text: Increased sexual desire. Gangrene of genital parts. Sticking pain above r. pubis, extending along spermatic cord, while lying and on moving about; > when sitting up. Voluptuous itching beneath prepuce with desire for coition.

female.text: Catamenia too early and too abundant blood thin; with nightly tearing in vertex. Menorrhagia, blood dark, in large clots, during climaxis. (Metrorrhagia in tumour case.). Tearing in head, odontalgia, and cuttings, during catamenia. Severe pain in sacral region extending to pubis with dizziness and dimness of vision; cold extremities; cold tongue; great melancholy (dysmenorrhœa). Dysmenorrhœa; colic first day, headache second day all over head, with or without sick feeling. (Relieves the pain of uterine and rectal cancer.). Attacks of suffocation, with palpitation and a sort of gasping for breath; must lie down sometimes to find relief (pregnancy). Burning and stinging in and below mammæ. Needle-like stitches in and beneath l. breast. Creeping or moving stitches in female breast, whereby the skin becomes sensitive to touch lasting a long time.

Menses : too early and too profuse ; blood thin ; with nightly tearing in vertex. Menorrhagia, blood dark, in large clots, during climaxis. Severe pain in sacral region, extending to pubis ; frontal headache, with dizziness and dimness of vision ; coldness of extremities ; icy coldness of tongue ; great melancholy. θ Dysmenorrhœa. Burning and stinging in and below mamma. PREGNANCY. PARTURITION. LACTATION. [24] [Laur.]. Attacks of suffocation, with palpitation and a sort of gasping for breath ; must lie down sometimes to find relief. θ Pregnancy. She is conscious of a shock passing through whole body before spasm. θ Puerperal convulsions.

modalities.text: REST. POSITION. MOTION. [35] [Laur.]. When lying down : coughs continuously ; ends of fingers and toes knobby. Must lie down : suffocating spell about heart. Cannot lie down : heart disease. When she attempts to rise from a recumbent posture, sensation as of a heavy lump falling from pit of stomach to back. Stooping : beating in head < ; cough <. Compelled to bend head forward : pressure in nape of neck. Sitting posture : gasps for breath ; feet go to sleep. After rising from seat : stiffness of feet. Crossing legs : feet go to sleep. Motion : cough < ; causes pain in external thorax. A little exercise produces gasping for breath. Afternoon : colicky pains ; chill, coldness and shivering. After dinner : irresistible sleepiness. Evening : headache > ; cough < ; irresistible sleepiness ; chill, coldness and shivering. Night : tearing in vertex ; when lying down, coughs continuously. Open air : vertigo < ; coldness in forehead and vertex > ; pressure in nape of neck <. In room : coldness in forehead and vertex <. On approaching stove : nausea ; cold. External warmth : does not > coldness. When touched by water : burning between fingers.
